I read the prompt and the three prior investigations as requested. The prompt‚Äôs format fixes distinguished tame generators sigma,tau, with implicit tau^sigma=tau^2, and requires later generators to lie in the pro-2 core; the prior file‚Äôs Turn 3 refutes the Turn 1/2 candidate by an unramified-quadratic subgroup test.The broader Jannsen‚ÄìWingberg strategy really does require checking Demu≈°kin structures over finite tame subextensions, not just the base maximal pro-2 quotient; Neukirch‚Äôs report explicitly describes that proof method as reducing to Demu≈°kin groups over finite tame subextensions, with class-field-theoretic structure and group action data.Numdam Jarden‚ÄìShusterman also record that the known explicit p‚âÝ2 description and Diekert‚Äôs dyadic case do not cover K=Q2.TAU Mathematics The Turn 3 obstruction is correct. Killing œÑ, the Turn 1/2 relation becomesrold=x2y3yœÉ.For the unramified quadratic subgroup U, with Schreier generatorsx0,y0,x1,y1,S=œÉ2,the bad index-2 subgroup œÜ(x0)=œÜ(x1)=1,¬ÝœÜ(y0)=œÜ(y1)=œÜ(S)=0 has the 2-adic Smith factor 8. So Vab contains Z/8, impossible for a quadratic extension of the unramified quadratic 2-adic field. That is not a cosmetic failure; it is exactly the missing cyclotomic-orientation invariant.The useful new point is that the old candidate used the wrong arithmetic lift of the Labute generator. In the normal dyadic Demu≈°kin relationa2b4 [b,c]=1,the element a is not the inertia element corresponding to ‚àí1. Abelianizing gives 2a+4b=0,so the actual order-two inertia class is t=a+2b.

If b is the unramified generator, then one should put a=xb‚àí2, with x the wild/inertia generator corresponding to ‚àí1. Substitution gives(xb‚àí2)2b4 [b,y]=xb‚àí2xb2 [b,y].Thus the corrected base maximal-pro-2 relation, with z denoting the pro-2 unramified generator, isrbase=xz‚àí2xz2 [z,y]=xz‚àí2xz2zyz‚àí1y‚àí1.This fixes precisely the Turn 3 failure. For the same unramified quadratic subgroup U, the rewritten relators arer0=x0S‚àí1x0Sy1y0‚àí1,r1=x1S‚àí1x1S2y0S‚àí1y1‚àí1.

Their abelianized matrix in the basis x0,y0,x1,y1,S is(20‚àí11021‚àí100),whose 2-adic Smith form has one factor 2, soUab‚âÉZ23‚äïZ/2.For Turn 3‚Äôs previously bad index-2 subgroup, the Smith factors become 1,1,1,4,soVab‚âÉZ25‚äïZ/4,not Z25‚äïZ/8. Enumerating all 15 index-2 subgroups of this U gives exactly 14 with torsion Z/2 and one with torsion Z/4, matching the unique quadratic extension obtained by adjoining i, and with no Z/8.So the old candidate should be replaced at least at the base-pro-2 level by sigma,tau,x,y r_base_after_tau_killed = x*sigma^-2*x*sigma^2*sigma*y*sigma^-1*y^-1 where this line is only describing the œÑ=1 maximal pro-2 specialization.The obvious tame lift is then not the Turn 1/2 relation. A first repaired lift would be sigma,tau,x,y n = (y*tau)^40491355905 x*sigma^-2*x*sigma^2*n^sigma*y^-1 using 40491355905 for the idempotent œÄ2. Its linear term over a finite tame quotientŒîe,f=‚ü®œÉÀâ,œÑÀâ‚à£œÑÀâe=1,œÉÀâf=1,œÉÀâœÑÀâœÉÀâ‚àí1=œÑÀâ2‚ü©is‚Ñìg=Xg+XgœÉ‚àí2+Yg+i=0‚àëe‚àí1YgœÉœÑi.The Y-part is the same norm operator that made the earlier rank calculation work, and the full map still has one-dimensional kernel generated by the total norm ‚àëŒ¥‚ààŒî Œ¥. Thus it givesdimF2H1=m+2,m=ef,for all finite tame (e,f).However, this naive lift is not yet a final answer. A stronger unramified-degree-5 test exposes a new defect: for f=5, the relation with the literal exponent 2 in the correction term gives 127 index-2 subgroups, but the torsion distribution in their abelianizations is not the local-field distribution 126 copies of Z/2 and one copy of Z/4. Instead, the computation produces extra torsion-free and (Z/2)2 -torsion cases. That means the correction ‚ÄúxœÉ‚àí2xœÉ2 ‚Äù is the right local/base idea, but the global tame-compatible correction must be a more delicate profinite or orbit-dependent expression, not the scalar integer 2.A useful numerical clue: replacing the scalar 2 by the integer 41 in the unramified-only familyxœÉ‚àíkxœÉk [œÉ,y]passes the full index-2 torsion distribution for unramified degrees f=2,3,4,5. I do not yet have a derivation of 41, and many finite tests can be misleading; the point is that the missing term appears to be a profinite exponent/transfer correction controlling unramified odd-degree subgroups.

ESTABLISHED: Turn 3‚Äôs refutation is valid. The correct base-level repair is to replace the old x2 -term by a shifted torsion-inertia term xœÉ‚àí2xœÉ2, giving the base relationxœÉ‚àí2xœÉ2 [œÉ,y]=1.This fixes the unramified-quadratic Z/8 obstruction and gives the correct 15-subgroup torsion distribution over the unramified quadratic field.

GAP: The naive full tame lift with scalar exponent 2 still fails an unramified-degree-5 orientation test. The remaining missing object is a global profinite/orbit correction replacing the scalar 2 in xœÉ‚àí2xœÉ2, compatible with all unramified and tamely ramified finite quotients.

NEXT: Derive that correction from local class field transfer of the ‚àí1 unit through unramified degree f extensions, then express it in the prompt syntax, likely as a norm-like word rather than a plain integer exponent.[BREAKTHROUGH]
